Name: v2: NakedClaude Demo
Description: Rulebook generated from Airtable base 'v2: NakedClaude Demo'.

schema.name: FullName
schema.explanation_rich: **The smallest chain in the rulebook — v2's opening DAG edge.** v1 had a single calculated boolean off one raw field; v2 stacks two calculated fields on two raw fields. `FullName = FirstName & " " & LastName`, and then the slug `Name` is computed *from* `FullName` via `SUBSTITUTE(LOWER({{FullName}}), " ", "-")`. Worked example: `FirstName = "Alice"`, `LastName = "Johnson"` → `FullName = "Alice Johnson"` → `Name = "alice-johnson"`. Edit `FirstName` to `Alicia` and both downstream values recompute in every substrate on the next read — no glue code.

schema.name: StatusIsBlocking
schema.explanation_rich: **v2's headline capability: a lookup that reaches across a foreign key.** `Customers.Status` is an FK into `Statuses`; this field uses Excel-dialect `INDEX(Statuses!{{IsBlocking}}, MATCH(Customers!{{Status}}, Statuses!{{StatuseId}}, 0))` to read the target row's `IsBlocking` flag *as if it were a local column*. Worked example: Alice's `Status = "pending"`; the `pending` row in `Statuses` has `IsBlocking = TRUE`; so Alice's `StatusIsBlocking` is `TRUE` — the same value every substrate reads. Flip `pending.IsBlocking` to `FALSE` in `Statuses` and Alice's lookup flips with it on the next read, no customer-row edit required.

schema.name: IsStopped
schema.explanation_rich: **A composite rule that mixes a lookup result with a raw field.** `IsStopped = IF(AND({{StatusIsBlocking}}, {{FirstName}} = "Bob"), TRUE(), FALSE())`. This is v2's first *multi-input* business rule: one input comes across the FK lookup chain (`StatusIsBlocking`), the other is a local raw field (`FirstName`). Worked example: of v2's five customers, only **Bob** is both named `"Bob"` *and* sitting in a blocking status (`on-hold`), so only Bob's `IsStopped` is `TRUE`. Rename Bob to `Robert` *or* flip `on-hold.IsBlocking` to `FALSE` and the same rule, evaluated in every substrate, drops Bob out of the stopped set.

schema.name: OwnerEmail
schema.Description: Email address of the user who owns this customer record. Used by Row-Level Security to scope visibility to the JWT email of the requester.

data.Name: bob-
data.StatusDescription: This status indicates the customer or request is on hold and temporarily paused.

data.Name: alice-johnson
data.StatusDescription: This status shows the customer or request is pending further action or review.

data.Name: brian-lee
data.StatusDescription: This status indicates a newly created customer or request that has not yet been processed.

data.Name: carla-smith
data.StatusDescription: This status is used when the customer or request has been delayed and is not progressing as scheduled.

data.Name: caroline-
data.StatusDescription: This status means the customer or request is currently being processed.

Customers.summary_rich: The **central table** in v2. Each row carries a foreign key to `Statuses` and an `OwnerEmail` for Row-Level Security. v1 only had raw fields and a single boolean calc; v2 stacks a real chain on top — raw `FirstName` + `LastName` → calculated `FullName` → calculated slug `Name`, *and* a lookup chain that reaches across the FK into `Statuses` (`StatusDisplayName`, `StatusIsBlocking`, `StatusDescription`). The whole DAG fires identically in every substrate.

schema.name: OwnerEmail
schema.Description: Email address of the user who owns this status. Used by Row-Level Security to scope visibility.

data.Name: delayed
data.Description: This status is used when the customer or request has been delayed and is not progressing as scheduled.

data.Name: cancelled
data.Description: This status means the customer or request has been cancelled and will not proceed further.

data.Name: processing
data.Description: This status means the customer or request is currently being processed.

data.Name: new
data.Description: This status indicates a newly created customer or request that has not yet been processed.

data.Name: in-review
data.Description: This status is for customers or requests that are currently under review.

data.Name: pending
data.Description: This status shows the customer or request is pending further action or review.

data.Name: on-hold
data.Description: This status indicates the customer or request is on hold and temporarily paused.

Statuses.summary_rich: The **lookup target**. `Customers.Status` is a foreign-key relationship into this table; v2's lookup fields (`StatusDisplayName`, `StatusIsBlocking`, `StatusDescription`) pull values across that FK using Excel-dialect `INDEX(...,MATCH(...))`. Flipping `IsBlocking` on a row here propagates through the lookup into every customer that points at this status — and, via the composite rule `IsStopped`, can re-decide whether a customer is stopped without touching the customer row at all.

schema.name: EmailAddress
schema.Description: Primary identity — matches the JWT email of the signed-in user.

schema.name: Role
schema.Description: admin = full access; customer = sees only owned customers (RLS DAG).

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Airtable export with schema-first type mapping: Schemas, Data, Relationships (FK links), Lookups (INDEX/MATCH), Aggregations (SUMIFS/COUNTIFS/Rollups), and Calculated fields (formulas) in Excel dialect. Field types are determined from Airtable's schema metadata FIRST (no coercion), with intelligent fallback to formula/data analysis only when schema is unavailable.

_conversion_metadata.field_type_mapping: checkbox→boolean, number→number/integer, multipleRecordLinks→relationship, multipleLookupValues→lookup, rollup→aggregation, count→aggregation, formula→calculated

type_inference.priority: airtable_metadata (NO COERCION) → formula_analysis → reference_resolution → data_analysis (fallback only)
type_inference.airtable_type_mapping: checkbox→boolean, singleLineText→string, multilineText→string, number→number/integer, datetime→datetime, singleSelect→string, email→string, url→string, phoneNumber→string
type_inference.data_coercion_hierarchy: Only used as fallback when Airtable schema unavailable: datetime → number → integer → boolean → base64 → json → string

_meta.tagline: Two tables joined by an FK — and a rule that fires across the join.
_meta.description_rich: NakedClaude **v2** is the second rung of a four-rung ladder. v1 had one entity and one calculated field. v2 adds a *second* entity — `Statuses` — and wires `Customers` to it through a foreign key. That single FK unlocks three new rulebook capabilities: **relationships** (`Customer.Status` → `Statuses`), **lookups** (`StatusDisplayName`, `StatusIsBlocking`, `StatusDescription`, all via Excel-dialect `INDEX`/`MATCH`), and **chained calculated fields** (raw → `FullName` → slug `Name`). On top of that, v2 introduces an `OwnerEmail` column on every business entity plus an `AppUsers` table — the *scaffolding* for Row-Level Security that v3 and v4 will exercise more fully. The headline rule is `IsStopped`: a customer is stopped when their *status* is blocking **and** their first name is `"Bob"`. One side of that AND comes across the join; the other is local. Same answer in Postgres, Python, Excel, and OWL.
use_cases: **Reach across a foreign key without writing a JOIN.** Open Alice (`Status = pending`) and see `StatusDisplayName`, `StatusIsBlocking`, and `StatusDescription` already populated from the `Statuses` row — every substrate computed them from the FK, no application code.

**Flip a flag in one table, watch dependent customers re-decide.** Change `pending.IsBlocking` from `TRUE` to `FALSE` in `Statuses` — Alice's `StatusIsBlocking` flips on the next read, and any `IsStopped` rule that depended on it re-evaluates.

**Stack two calculated fields on two raw fields.** Edit `Carla` → `Smith` to `Carla` → `Smithson` and watch `FullName` recompute to `Carla Smithson`, then `Name` recompute to `carla-smithson` — the rulebook's first real DAG edge.

**Combine a lookup result with a local field in one rule.** `IsStopped = AND(StatusIsBlocking, FirstName = "Bob")` mixes a cross-join lookup with a local raw value — only Bob (on-hold + named Bob) lights up.
**Scaffold for RLS without enforcing it yet.** Every business row carries an `OwnerEmail`; `AppUsers` carries email + role. v2 records the ownership; v3/v4 enforce it.
_meta.journal_seed: Five customers, seven statuses, one foreign key wiring them together. Bob is the only one stopped — he's both named `Bob` and sitting in a blocking status. The rule that decides that mixes a value from across the join with a value from the same row.
